name: Pacific Heights
desc: Ridgetop homes with panoramic city-to-ocean views — winding roads, cooler mountain air, very private and exclusive.
insider: Puu Ualakaa State Park (Round Top Drive) has free parking and the best panoramic photo spot in all of Honolulu
Roads are single-lane and winding — not great for large vehicles or nervous drivers, and no sidewalks
At night you can see the entire Waikiki skyline lit up — multiple pulloffs along Round Top Drive
The privacy and seclusion up here make it feel miles from downtown despite being 10 minutes away
Some homes have unobstructed views from Diamond Head to Pearl Harbor — the reason prices top $3M
Delivery drivers and rideshares often get lost on the narrow switchbacks — give very specific directions
The Norfolk pine trees lining the roads are massive and historic — some date to early 1900s plantings
Pacific Heights was home to several prominent missionary families — historic plaques dot the older estates
No restaurants or shops up here — you're driving down to Nuuanu or downtown for everything
Morning fog is common and burns off by 10am — the reveal of the view beneath is spectacular
